section: Chapter 3: Body movements musculo skeletal system

concepts.lesson: Muscle fibers are classified into two main types: red muscle fibers and white muscle fibers. Red muscle fibers contain more myoglobin, which gives them a reddish color and allows them to store more oxygen. They primarily use aerobic respiration to generate energy, making them highly efficient for endurance activities like long-distance running.
key_points: Red muscle fibers have more myoglobin than white muscle fibers
Red muscle fibers are well-suited for endurance activities due to their ability to store oxygen
White muscle fibers generate energy anaerobically and fatigue faster

concepts.lesson: The skeletal system provides support, protection, and movement to the body. It consists of bones, cartilage, and ligaments that work together to maintain posture, facilitate movement, and provide a framework for muscles to attach.
key_points: The skeletal system provides support and protection to the body
It facilitates movement by allowing muscles to attach to bones
It maintains posture and provides a framework for muscles

concepts.lesson: There are three main types of movement: flexion, extension, and rotation. Flexion is the movement towards the midline of the body, while extension is the movement away from it. Rotation is the movement around a fixed axis.
key_points: Flexion is the movement towards the midline of the body
Extension is the movement away from the midline of the body
Rotation is the movement around a fixed axis

concepts.lesson: Muscles attach to bones at specific points called tendons. These attachments allow muscles to contract and move the body.
key_points: Muscles attach to bones at tendons
Tendons are made of dense connective tissue

concepts.lesson: Bones can be classified into several types based on their shape and function. Long bones, for example, have a shaft (diaphysis) and two ends (epiphyses). Short bones are cube-shaped and have equal dimensions in all directions.
key_points: Long bones have a shaft and two ends
Short bones are cube-shaped with equal dimensions

concepts.lesson: There are three main types of joints: ball-and-socket, hinge, and pivot. Ball-and-socket joints allow for movement in all directions, while hinge joints allow for flexion and extension only.
key_points: Ball-and-socket joints allow for movement in all directions
Hinge joints allow for flexion and extension only
